macro_summary: US equity futures are lower to start the week as US-Iran peace talks hit a fresh snag on their first day, with oil prices rising and President Trump threatening further military action — reopening Strait of Hormuz uncertainty. The dominant macro overhang remains the Fed: Chair Warsh's inaugural FOMC meeting last week resulted in a hold at 3.5%-3.75%, but with nine of 18 officials now penciling in at least one rate hike in 2026 and the easing bias stripped from the statement, the rate-cut narrative is dead. The week's decisive data event is May PCE (the Fed's preferred inflation gauge) due Thursday, June 25 — a hot print could materially reprice hike odds and hit rate-sensitive equities. Today's calendar is light, making it a setup week rather than an action week; the highest-conviction near-term events are the Micron and FedEx earnings prints on Wednesday and Tuesday nights respectively.
key_events: US-Iran peace talks resume but stumble on Day 1; oil pops, US futures lower
Trump threatens fresh strikes on Hezbollah; Strait of Hormuz reopening remains fragile
Fed hold at 3.5%-3.75% now fully digested; dot plot signals hike risk by year-end 2026
Warsh removes easing bias from FOMC statement; 2-yr Treasury yields at 1-yr highs
May PCE inflation print due Thursday June 25 — critical read for rate trajectory
FedEx (FDX) Q4 FY2026 earnings after close Tuesday June 23
Micron (MU) Q3 FY2026 earnings after close Wednesday June 24
Intel (INTC) +10.6% last Thursday on Trump-announced Apple foundry deal; stock now above consensus analyst target
No major US economic data scheduled for today (Monday June 22)

ideas.name: Micron Technology, Inc.
ideas.thesis: Micron reports fiscal Q3 2026 earnings Wednesday June 24 after close — the single highest-stakes semiconductor print of the week. The company guided Q3 to record revenue of ~$33.5B and ~81% gross margin, and the Street has been steadily ratcheting estimates higher into the print. Micron's Q2 beat was extraordinary: revenue up 196% YoY, EPS of $12.20 beating consensus by 33% — its largest beat in nearly three years. The AI HBM memory cycle is the core driver, with Micron's entire HBM3E capacity reported as sold out through the end of 2026 and long-term volume agreements now signed with the majority of core hyperscaler customers.
ideas.catalyst: Q3 FY2026 earnings report after market close on Wednesday June 24, 2026 — confirmed date. Key watch items: HBM4 allocation commentary, Q4 guidance, and any FY2027 CAPEX/margin signals.
ideas.edge: Consensus Q3 EPS estimate ~$19.72-$19.95 on ~$34.4-34.7B revenue. Company's own guidance midpoint implies ~$19.15 EPS — meaning Street is already above guidance, yet the last two quarters saw 33%+ and 27%+ surprise beats. Full-year FY2026 EPS now projected at ~$57.71, up 651% from FY2025's $7.68. FY2027 P/E trades below 10x at current price (~$1,134). TD Cowen price target $1,500; Aletheia Capital $1,600 — implying 32-41% upside from last close. Options volatility elevated ahead of print per Seeking Alpha screener.
ideas.bull_case: If Micron delivers another outsized beat and raises Q4 guidance above consensus, with HBM4 allocation secured into 2027, the stock could re-rate sharply higher as FY2027 sub-10x P/E becomes the anchor. Long-term pricing agreements transform Micron from a cyclical to a secular growth story in the market's eyes.
ideas.bear_case: The stock is up ~297% YTD and trades at 185% above GuruFocus intrinsic value. Any guidance miss, softening in HBM demand from hyperscalers (e.g. AI efficiency gains reducing memory requirements), or compression of 81% gross margins would hit a very stretched valuation hard. SK Hynix holds ~62% HBM market share and remains ahead on per-pin HBM4 speed. Heavy insider selling ($92.5M in past 3 months) is an additional caution flag.

ideas.name: FedEx Corporation
ideas.thesis: FedEx reports Q4 FY2026 earnings after close Tuesday June 23 — the first quarterly print following the June 1 completion of the FedEx Freight spin-off (FDXF). This is a structural inflection point: the leaner, Express-focused FedEx enters FY2027 with its Network 2.0 transformation targeting over $2B in permanent cost savings by 2029. Management raised full-year EPS guidance to $19.30-$20.10 after last quarter's 27% beat. The Zacks Earnings ESP is +3.76% (most-accurate estimate above consensus), and FedEx has beaten EPS estimates in each of its last four quarters.
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings after market close Tuesday June 23, 2026 — confirmed. Key focus: FY2027 guidance under the new post-Freight structure, and the monetization timeline for FedEx's retained 19.9% FDXF stake.
ideas.edge: FedEx has beaten consensus EPS in 4 consecutive quarters, with the Q3 beat coming in at +26.81% ($5.25 actual vs $4.09 expected). The Zacks Most Accurate Estimate is above consensus, yielding a +3.76% ESP — historically a strong predictor of beats. FY2027 EPS consensus is $22.04, implying ~11.8% growth. Stock up ~80% over 52 weeks, outperforming the S&P 500's ~31% and XLI's ~34% by wide margins — driven by Network 2.0 savings credibility rather than just macro tailwinds.
ideas.bull_case: A clean beat plus upside FY2027 guidance — specifically evidence that Network 2.0 savings are tracking ahead of schedule and Express margins are expanding — could re-rate the stock as investors gain clarity on the standalone economics of the slimmer FDX.
ideas.bear_case: The stock is trading near its 52-week high after a massive run, meaning expectations are elevated and a 'sell the news' reaction is plausible even on a moderate beat. Q4 EPS is already expected to decline ~2.6% YoY due to spin-off costs and the MD-11 fleet grounding (~$55M Q4 headwind). Any revenue miss or cautious FY2027 language — especially citing Middle East volume softness — could pressure shares meaningfully from current levels.

ideas.name: ProShares UltraShort 20+ Year Treasury ETF
ideas.thesis: The macro backdrop has shifted decisively hawkish: the Fed stripped its easing bias last week, nine of 18 FOMC members now project at least one rate hike in 2026, the 2-year Treasury yield hit its highest level in over a year, and the Fed raised its own 2026 inflation forecast to 3.6% headline / 3.3% core. Thursday's May PCE print — with the prior reading at 3.8% YoY and CPI already at 4.2% in May — is the next live trigger. A hot PCE would further cement hike expectations and push long-end yields higher, directly benefiting a leveraged short-Treasury position. TBT (2x inverse long-dated Treasuries) is the simplest liquid expression of this rate-risk hedge.
ideas.catalyst: May PCE inflation data release Thursday June 25, 2026. The Fed's own updated projections show median year-end funds rate at 3.8% — above current levels — signaling at least one hike is on the table. Next FOMC meeting is the next discrete decision point.
ideas.edge: CME FedWatch shows zero rate cuts priced for 2026 — a complete reversal from expectations of two cuts at the start of the year. Former Fed officials surveyed by Hilsenrath found 17 of 32 believe a hike is appropriate in 2026, with a median PCE forecast of 3.5% for year-end vs the Fed's 2% target. TBT benefits from yield curve repricing if PCE prints above consensus; the 2-yr note is already at a 1-yr high, and further inflation surprise has asymmetric upside for this instrument.
ideas.bull_case: A hot May PCE (above 3.8% YoY) combined with ongoing Middle East energy uncertainty could force the market to price in a 2026 hike more aggressively, pushing long-end yields higher and delivering a meaningful TBT gain over a 1-2 week window around the data release.
ideas.bear_case: If PCE comes in softer than feared — helped by declining gasoline prices following the partial Strait of Hormuz reopening — the bond market could rally sharply, punishing leveraged short-Treasury positions. TBT also has significant daily compounding decay risk as a 2x instrument, making it a poor multi-month hold.
